test_cases.title: Current active API version returns valid response without deprecation headers
preconditions: At least one current (non-deprecated, non-sunset) API version exists and is deployed (e.g. /v2/)
A valid endpoint exists under the current version (e.g. /v2/resources)
steps: Send a GET request to a known endpoint under the current active version, e.g. GET /v2/resources
Capture the HTTP response status code and headers
test_cases.expected_result: The response returns HTTP 2xx with a valid response body, and neither a 'Deprecation' header nor an 'X-API-Sunset-Date' header is present.

test_cases.title: Deprecated but still active API version returns valid response with Deprecation and X-API-Sunset-Date headers
preconditions: At least one deprecated but still active API version exists and is deployed (e.g. /v1/)
A valid endpoint exists under that deprecated version (e.g. /v1/resources)
The API is configured to treat this version as deprecated
steps: Send a GET request to a known endpoint under the deprecated active version, e.g. GET /v1/resources
Capture the HTTP response status code, body, and all response headers
test_cases.expected_result: The response returns HTTP 2xx with a valid response body, and both the 'Deprecation' header and the 'X-API-Sunset-Date' header are present in the response.

test_cases.title: Deprecated API version Deprecation header contains a non-empty value
preconditions: A deprecated but still active API version exists (e.g. /v1/)
A valid endpoint exists under that version (e.g. /v1/resources)
steps: Send a GET request to a known endpoint under the deprecated version, e.g. GET /v1/resources
Inspect the value of the 'Deprecation' response header
Inspect the value of the 'X-API-Sunset-Date' response header
test_cases.expected_result: Both the 'Deprecation' header and the 'X-API-Sunset-Date' header are present and contain non-empty values.

test_cases.title: Request to an unsupported API version returns HTTP 404
preconditions: The version prefix used in the request (e.g. /v999/) does not correspond to any deployed or deprecated version
steps: Send a GET request using a nonexistent version prefix, e.g. GET /v999/resources
Capture the HTTP response status code

test_cases.title: Request to a completely malformed version prefix returns HTTP 404
preconditions: The path used does not match any valid version pattern (e.g. /vX/, /version1/, /0/)
steps: Send a GET request with a malformed version prefix, e.g. GET /vX/resources
Capture the HTTP response status code

test_cases.title: Request to a formerly active but now fully removed (sunset) API version returns HTTP 404
preconditions: A previously deployed API version has been fully removed and is no longer active (e.g. /v0/)
The version is not configured as deprecated-but-active
steps: Send a GET request to a known endpoint path using the removed version prefix, e.g. GET /v0/resources
Capture the HTTP response status code
test_cases.expected_result: The response returns HTTP 404, with no valid response body for the resource.

assumptions: A 'current' (non-deprecated) version exists alongside at least one deprecated version for testing contrast between header presence and absence.
The 'Deprecation' header format and the 'X-API-Sunset-Date' date format are not specified in the requirement; tests only assert presence and non-emptiness of these headers.
Versioning applies uniformly to all endpoints under a given version prefix, so any representative endpoint can be used to verify versioning behavior.
A version that has passed its sunset date and is fully removed is considered 'unsupported' and should return HTTP 404, not a deprecated response.
notes: The requirement does not specify: (1) the exact format or allowed values for the 'Deprecation' header (e.g. boolean 'true', a date string, or a URL); (2) the date format for 'X-API-Sunset-Date'; (3) whether the 404 for unsupported versions should include a structured error body; (4) whether versioning applies only to certain resource paths or all paths; (5) how the transition from 'deprecated' to 'unsupported' is managed operationally. Coverage gaps exist around performance under version routing, behavior when the version prefix is present but the sub-path does not exist, and authentication/authorization interactions with versioned endpoints.
